channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: Peppa Pig and Transformers owner Hasbro hit by cyber-attack
item.description: [BBC Technology] The firm says its operations remain open but says the hack "may result in some delays".

item.title: The real impact of AI on budgets, stacks and teams
item.description: [MarTech] AI is replacing tools and reshaping budgets, but it’s also expanding stacks and increasing headcount. The post The real impact of AI on budgets, stacks and teams appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Agentic AI discovery requires machine-readable brands
item.description: [MarTech] Drive better visibility and outcomes in AI search by making your brand easier for machines to interpret, connect and act on. The post Agentic AI discovery requires machine-readable brands appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Claude Code users hitting usage limits 'way faster than expected'
item.description: [BBC Technology] Anthropic, the company behind the AI coding assistant, said it was fixing a problem blocking users.

item.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
item.description: [BBC Technology] Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran’s Threats on US Tech, Trump’s Plans for Midterms, and Polymarket’s Pop-up Flop
item.description: [WIRED AI] In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.

item.title: OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
item.description: [WIRED AI] OpenAI is acquiring TBPN, a business talk show that’s popular among Silicon Valley elites, as it continues to battle its negative public image.

item.title: The latest AI-powered martech news and releases
item.description: [MarTech] AI chatbots are now ignoring instructions and going off-script — because apparently even automation tools can get bored with their jobs. The post The latest AI-powered martech news and releases appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Fewer UK adults posting on social media, Ofcom finds
item.description: [BBC Technology] Some experts believe it highlights a social media shift as platforms boost short video.

item.title: HubSpot moves to outcome-based pricing for some Breeze AI agents
item.description: [MarTech] HubSpot bets customers will be more likely to deploy its AI agents if they only pay when the agent does its job. The post HubSpot moves to outcome-based pricing for some Breeze AI agents appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: As AI does more of the work, are we building the right leaders?
item.description: [MarTech] AI is accelerating analysis and reporting, but fewer marketers understand the data underneath. That gap is where leadership risk begins. The post As AI does more of the work, are we building the right leaders? appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to be worth $1 trillion with planned public listing
item.description: [BBC Technology] The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

item.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] AI skeptics aren’t the only ones warning users not to unthinkingly trust models’ outputs — that’s what the AI companies say themselves in their terms of service.

item.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
item.description: [The Verge AI] AI music platform Suno's policy is that it does not permit the use of copyrighted material. You can upload your own tracks to remix or set your original lyrics to AI-generated music. But, it's supposed to recognize and stop you from using other people's songs and lyrics. Now, no system is perfect, b

item.title: Can orbital data centers help justify a massive valuation for SpaceX?
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we debated Elon Musk's vision for data centers in space.

item.title: In Japan, the robot isn’t coming for your job; it’s filling the one nobody wants
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Driven by labor shortages, Japan is pushing physical AI from pilot projects into real-world deployment.

item.title: I let Gemini in Google Maps plan my day and it went surprisingly well
item.description: [The Verge AI] You may be familiar with Gemini as the thing that's in every Google service you use - whether you want it or not. While it's been a constant, sometimes unwelcome presence in Gmail for at least the past year, it's a relatively new addition to Maps. And you know what? It's kind of great. To […]

item.title: Grammarly’s sloppelganger saga
item.description: [The Verge AI] This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the ups and downs of AI, follow Stevie Bonifield. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here. How it started Most people probably know Grammarly

item.title: 36氪首发 | 清华系团队研发矿石AI智能分选机，完成近2亿元C轮融资
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨欧雪 编辑丨袁斯来 硬氪获悉，矿石AI智能分选设备企业——北京霍里思特科技有限公司（下称“霍里思特”）已完成近2亿元C轮融资，由招商局资本领投，京国瑞、大兴产投、北创投、开源思创及初辉资本跟投。本轮资金将主要用于核心技术研发、全球化业务拓展及团队建设。 霍里思特成立于2010年，是国内在全矿种（有色、黑色、非金属、放射性及煤矿）均有智能分选大量落地应用的设备供应商。公司核心团队源自清华大学精密仪器系国家重点实验室，创始人郭劲为清华精密仪器系硕士，曾任职GE医疗CT部门。公司在X射线光谱分析与AI智能识别算法领域有近二十年科研积累。 硬氪获悉，传统选矿主要依赖人工手选或湿法

item.title: Britain woos Anthropic expansion after US defence clash: Report
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: 锤爆苹果壁垒：AI成最强攻城锤
item.description: [钛媒体] 网络安全正在从“人与人的对抗”转向“智能体与智能体的对抗”。

item.title: 清明节特辑：过去这一年，哪些互联网产品先走一步
item.description: [钛媒体] 这些产品的倒下不仅是生意的失败，更是AI大潮下，一场关于“效率”与“生存”的残酷大洗牌。

item.title: 高端智能投影品牌AWOL Vision获近亿元B轮融资，新品拿下近2000万美金众筹｜硬氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 作者｜黄楠 编辑｜袁斯来 硬氪获悉，高端智能投影品牌AWOL Vision近日完成近亿元B轮融资，投资方包括天堂硅谷、会畅科技及金鹏佳等机构。资金将主要用于核心技术研发，持续迭代超短焦与长焦激光显示能力，并推进“家庭AI智能娱乐平台”生态构建；同时加快全球渠道与品牌拓展，强化供应链协同，并引入顶尖人才以夯实全球化运营基础。 AWOL Vision成立于2020年，长期专注RGB激光投影与家庭视听体验创新，并以深圳海高特科技有限公司作为其研发与设计中枢，为全球视听领域用户提供更具沉浸感的解决方案，重塑家庭娱乐体验。 3月31日，AWOL Vision新款超短焦4K三色激光投影产品

item.title: “AI预测未来”！碾压马斯克Grok-4！国产 AI登顶全球
item.description: [钛媒体] 马斯克曾公开断言：“预测未来的能力，是对模型智能性最好的测试。”

item.title: Apple at 50: Three products that changed how we live - and three that really didn't
item.description: [BBC Technology] On the tech giant's 50th year, we ask analysts to give their top three Apple successes and misses

item.title: 两次众筹破千万后，AWOL Vision还能“抢跑”多久？| 出海爆品先知
item.description: [钛媒体] AWOL Vision的两次千万美元级众筹，是一个美中创业团队在全球高端消费电子市场的一次成功“抢跑”。要从“抢跑”到“领跑”，Awol需要回答的问题还很多。

item.title: You may have heard of ‘frugal chic’. Is it just another way to justify buying something you don’t need?
item.description: [Straits Times] Whatever it is, it certainly boosted my motivation to continue being financially responsible.

item.title: Singapore’s 30 largest stocks gain 5% in first quarter of 2026, but Iran war keeps markets on edge
item.description: [Straits Times] Markets should continue to be volatile after the US President’s address to the nation revealed no clear end to the Iran war.

item.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
item.description: [The Verge AI] In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she'd never uploaded them to Spotify, and something was off about the vocals. She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the

item.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] It’s about to become more expensive for Claude Code subscribers to use Anthropic’s coding assistant with OpenClaw and other third-party tools.

item.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
item.description: [The Verge AI] "This looks like AI." It's a phrase I dread seeing as a writer who dabbles in illustration and amateur photography. In a world where generative AI technology is increasingly adept at mimicking the work of humans, people are naturally skeptical when online platforms refuse to label even obvious AI co

item.title: Singapore biotech firm Mirxes delays results in Hong Kong after audit flags issues
item.description: [Straits Times] KPMG identified concerns involving 14 transactions tied to US14.8 million in prepayments.

item.title: Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware
item.description: [WIRED AI] Plus: The FBI says a recent hack of its wiretap tools poses a national security risk, attackers stole Cisco source code as part of an ongoing supply chain hacking spree, and more.

item.title: Nasa astronauts reach safe orbit in historic moon mission
item.description: [Business Times] [AUSTIN] Nasa’s crew of astronauts launched to space and reached a stable orbit, kicking off a landmark journey that will take them...

item.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Glen Anderson, president of Rainmaker Securities, says the secondary market for private shares has never been more active — with Anthropic the hottest trade around, OpenAI losing ground, and SpaceX's looming IPO poised to reshape the landscape for everyone.

item.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
item.description: [The Verge AI] Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw," according to an email sent to users on Frid

item.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: [WIRED AI] Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

item.title: OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
item.description: [WIRED AI] The company is undergoing major leadership restructuring as its CEO of AGI deployment goes on leave for “several weeks.”

item.title: Your AI governance gap is bigger than you think
item.description: [MarTech] AI is already being used across your organization. Audit tools, set guardrails and reduce risk with a practical governance framework. The post Your AI governance gap is bigger than you think appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: 神州数码2025年营收超1400亿，AI相关业务增长近五成｜最前线
item.description: [36氪] 作者｜黄楠 编辑｜袁斯来 神州数码（000034.SZ）2025年全年业绩报告近日发布。 财报显示，公司全年实现营业收入1438亿元，同比增长12%，营收规模连续三年稳步攀升。其中，AI相关业务成为核心增长引擎，全年收入达330亿元，同比增长了48%，正式迈入规模化落地期，成为与传统分销业务并重的当期增长极。 具体到业务板块，高价值战略业务呈现爆发式增长。以神州问学为核心的AI软件及服务业务营收1.1亿元，同比增长了165.4%，企业级Agent中台进入商业化变现阶段；数云服务及软件业务营收35.6亿元，同比增长22%；自有品牌神州鲲泰算力产品营收同比增长62.4%达到74.4

item.title: The 3 Best Portable Jump Starters in 2026: Get Charged Up
item.description: [WIRED AI] The new crop of portable jump starters means you'll never be stuck waiting on a tow. Here are the devices we'd trust.

item.title: 阅文“AI拟真人大讲堂”：好故事才是AI时代的未来
item.description: [36氪] 如今内容行业风头正盛的莫过于AI拟真人剧。 它是从AI漫剧这一赛道中生长出来的内容形态，也是视频生成大模型飞跃式进步下的产物。 早在2025年下半年的时候，这一内容形态就已经崭露头角。彼时有一批从业者认为，AI拟真人剧的天花板相当于整个传统影视行业，要高于AI漫剧，于是在诸多技术问题尚未被解决的时候就决定入局。 今年1月以来，字节旗下Seedance2.0、生数科技Vidu Q3的发布则彻底引爆了这一赛道。人物表情的恐怖谷效应、角色难以保持的一致性，以及打斗场面不连贯等问题，都在底层大模型技术的进步中消融。 Vidu解决方案负责人郑重如此表示，视频生成大模型在过去一年中，从“老

item.title: 36氪首发丨前赛默飞员工创业黑灯实验室，实现国内100%交付，获近亿融资
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨欧雪 编辑丨袁斯来 硬氪获悉，北京戴纳实验科技股份有限公司（下称“戴纳科技”）已于近日完成近亿元B+轮融资。本轮由顺禧基金管理的北京市新材料产业投资基金（以下简称“北京新材料基金”）和北京市丰台区丰泽志远产业培育基金（以下简称“丰泽基金”）共同投资。资金将主要用于核心技术迭代、产能扩张，以及与北京化工大学联合搭建全球首个新材料CRO平台，加速AI+新材料研发服务的产业化落地。 戴纳科技成立于2011年，是国内首家提出并100%成功交付黑灯实验室的企业。公司专注于全球AI4S（AI for Science）基础设施的建设，通过构建以“AI+黑灯实验室”为基础的通用研发平台实现“

item.title: 36氪首发 | 00后上交医学博士做了款可穿戴止鼾设备，获得顺为独家投资
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 硬氪获悉，深圳夜合科技有限公司（下称“夜合科技”）近日宣布完成千万元天使轮融资，由顺为资本独家投资，深渡资本担任本轮融资的财务顾问。资金主要用于核心产品研发，加速“医疗技术向消费场景延伸”。 夜合科技成立于2025年，总部位于深圳。首款产品聚焦睡眠呼吸赛道，为鼾声人群和阻塞性睡眠呼吸暂停（OSA）患者开发轻量化、舒适、无体位限制的可穿戴颈部设备。 公司创始人冉昕昕拥有华西、瑞金两大顶级医院的医学背景，于上海交通大学攻读医学博士学位，长期专注于医工交叉领域的研究。在临床实践中，冉昕昕发现患者缺乏消费级止鼾
